svg.title: Charles Konzen — life timeline 1835–1903
svg.desc: Charles Konzen's 68 years across five chapters: Hessian youth, Atlantic crossing, Civil War service, St. Louis family life, and widowed late life. Major events are clickable.
svg.text: 68 years · five chapters · click any event
30 years · marriage, seven baptisms, watchman's work

a.title: 1859 — Atlantic crossing, Bremen → New York. Manifest not yet located.
7 January 1866 — Married Auguste Kreichelt at St. Marcus Evangelical Church, St. Louis.
26 January 1835 — Charles born in Hesse. Specific parish not yet recovered.

1880 U.S. Census — household at 1534 Jackson St., Charles "Gonson" age 39, cotton-press worker, wife Guissie. Separately, Pastor Nollau recorded daughter Bertha's 1880 baptism as "Jansen" — the Anglicization-bridge to Johnson.
1896 — Wife Auguste Kreichelt dies. Death record reads "Auguste Conson" — Charles is the surviving informant.
19 December 1903 — Charles dies of stomach cancer; buried Old Saint Marcus Cemetery.
20 December 1865 — Mustered out as Sergeant after three years' service.
ca. 1896-1903 — Louis Roth studio portrait taken (the only known photograph of Charles, recovered Sept 2025).
2 December 1862 — Enlisted in Battery B, 2nd Missouri Light Artillery, age 23.

1866-1880 — Seven children baptised at St. Marcus: Friedrich Wilhelm 1866-1867, Josephine Maria 1868-1890, Katherine Carolina 1870-1949, Auguste 1872-1890, Louise 1873-1947, Erwin Carl Konsen 1875-1950, Bertha 1879-1959.

g.text: Friedrich Wilhelm · Josephine Maria · Katherine Carolina · Auguste
click any tile to open · 6 events above the axis · 5 events below · symmetric spacing
